headline: From Amazon to Airbnb, US blue-chip bond sales flirt with record
summary: It barely seemed to matter for corporate borrowers this week as US investment-grade bond sales came close to yet another record.  Volumes reached $115 billion, just shy of the record weekly high of around $117 billion notched in 2020 when pandemic-related shutdowns and unprecedented measures by the Federal Reserve fueled a rush of capital raising by companies staring into the unknown.  “With plenty of dark clouds out there — geopolitics, rate volatility, private credit nerves — it’s impressive how smoothly IG is functioning,” said John Lloyd, global head of multisector credit and a portfolio manager at Janus Henderson Investors.

headline: Airbnb Remains Bullish on 'Reserve Now, Pay Later' as Adoption Builds, RBC Says
summary: Airbnb (ABNB) remains bullish on its "Reserve Now, Pay Later" feature, with early performance runnin
